title: Cloudsdale II-class Cruiser
content:
Spurred by the outbreak of the Great War, the Cloudsdale II-class was an invention of necessity. It was a refit and overhaul of the existing Cloudsdale-class colony ship, which enabled rapid conversion of existing spaceframes. The initial design was drafted in less than two weeks by Commander (later Admiral) Nebula Bolt during the retreat from Yildun Rest, following the destruction of the Equestrian colony there.

The Cloudsdale II was the first real warship class of the Equestrian navy, with the civilian infrastructure present on the previous colony ships stripped out and exchanged for additional hull plating and weapon systems. The first five ships of this class were directly refitted from the five Cloudsdale-class colony ships currently being constructed at the time of the designing of the Cloudsdale II-class. The Cloudsdale II were surprisingly effective in their desired role, serving as the backbone of the Equestrian fleet until the introduction of the larger Hurricane-Class.

Even after the creation of more advanced cruisers, the Cloudsdale II continued to serve, mostly assigned to patrol duty to the fringes of Equestrian space. The last Cloudsdale IIs known to serve in combat saw action in Luna's Rebellion against the Solar Empire.

Notable vessels

  • ESS Cloudsdale A: Launched 2429, Equus Orbital Yards. First ship of the class.
  • ESS Las Pegasus: Refit 2430, Equus Orbital Yards. Converted from active colony ship. Designated as museum ship in 2444.
